examples.title: Weighted Interval Scheduling DP
examples.description: Complete algorithm for WISP using dynamic programming with O(n log n) time
examples.code:
ALGORITHM WeightedIntervalScheduling(intervals)
BEGIN
  // Step 1: Sort intervals by finish time
  SORT intervals by finish_time
  
  // Step 2: Precompute finish times array
  n ← length(intervals)
  finish_times ← array of size n
  FOR i from 1 to n DO
    finish_times[i] ← intervals[i].finish
  END FOR
  
  // Step 3: Compute p(i) for each interval using binary search
  p ← array of size n
  FOR i from 1 to n DO
    p[i] ← BinarySearch(intervals[i].start, finish_times)
  END FOR
  
  // Step 4: Dynamic Programming
  DP ← array of size n+1
  DP[0] ← 0
  FOR i from 1 to n DO
    include_weight ← intervals[i].weight + DP[p[i]]
    DP[i] ← MAX(DP[i-1], include_weight)
  END FOR
  
  // Step 5: Reconstruct solution
  result ← empty list
  i ← n
  WHILE i > 0 DO
    include_weight ← intervals[i].weight + DP[p[i]]
    IF include_weight > DP[i-1] THEN
      ADD intervals[i] to result
      i ← p[i]
    ELSE
      i ← i - 1
    END IF
  END WHILE
  
  RETURN (DP[n], reverse(result))
END

FUNCTION BinarySearch(target, finish_times)
BEGIN
  low ← 1, high ← length(finish_times), result ← 0
  WHILE low ≤ high DO
    mid ← (low + high) / 2
    IF finish_times[mid] ≤ target THEN
      result ← mid
      low ← mid + 1
    ELSE
      high ← mid - 1
    END IF
  END WHILE
  RETURN result
END
examples.explanation: The algorithm sorts intervals by finish time, computes predecessor p(i) using binary search, builds DP table using recurrence DP[i] = max(DP[i-1], weight[i] + DP[p(i)]), and reconstructs solution by backtracking.

examples.title: WISP Implementation in Python
examples.description: Complete Python implementation with classes and detailed comments
examples.code:
class Interval:
    def __init__(self, start, end, weight):
        self.start = start
        self.end = end
        self.weight = weight

def weighted_interval_scheduling(intervals):
    if not intervals:
        return 0, []
    
    # Sort by finish time
    intervals.sort(key=lambda x: x.end)
    n = len(intervals)
    
    # Create finish times array (1-indexed for easier mapping)
    finish_times = [0] * (n + 1)
    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        finish_times[i] = intervals[i - 1].end
    
    # Compute p(i) using binary search
    p = [0] * (n + 1)
    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        p[i] = binary_search(finish_times, i, intervals[i - 1].start)
    
    # DP computation
    dp = [0] * (n + 1)
    for i in range(1, n + 1):
        include = intervals[i - 1].weight + dp[p[i]]
        dp[i] = max(dp[i - 1], include)
    
    # Reconstruct solution
    selected = []
    i = n
    while i > 0:
        include = intervals[i - 1].weight + dp[p[i]]
        if include > dp[i - 1]:
            selected.append(intervals[i - 1])
            i = p[i]
        else:
            i -= 1
    
    return dp[n], selected[::-1]

def binary_search(finish_times, n, target):
    low, high = 1, n
    result = 0
    while low <= high:
        mid = (low + high) // 2
        if finish_times[mid] <= target:
            result = mid
            low = mid + 1
        else:
            high = mid - 1
    return result

# Example usage
intervals = [
    Interval(0, 3, 5),
    Interval(2, 5, 6),
    Interval(4, 6, 4),
    Interval(5, 7, 2),
    Interval(6, 8, 8)
]
max_weight, selected = weighted_interval_scheduling(intervals)
print(f"Maximum Weight: {max_weight}")
print("Selected Intervals:")
for iv in selected:
    print(f"  ({iv.start}, {iv.end}, {iv.weight})")
examples.explanation: Python implementation demonstrates class-based interval representation, binary search for p(i), DP solution, and backtracking for reconstruction. Output for example gives max weight 13 with intervals (0,3,5) and (6,8,8).

examples.title: WISP Implementation in C
examples.description: Complete C implementation using structures and arrays
examples.explanation: C implementation uses qsort for sorting, manual binary search for p(i), iterative DP, and backtracking. The main function demonstrates with the example intervals yielding maximum weight 13.
